identity.canonical_title: How This Ties to TZP & Your 32/27 Scale Intuition: A(k)∝ r^α(k)

theory.canonical_statement: ## 8) How this ties to TZP & your 32/27 scale intuition - In your TZP fractal construction you used a contraction \(r=27/32\). That ratio can be introduced as a *scale-selection operator* on mode amplitudes: e.g., apply amplitude weighting \(A(k)\propto r^{\alpha(k)}\) across k shells to favor specific radial harmonics. This will bias the SBT power into bands that produce filament networks with fractal dimension controlled by \(\alpha\) — a direct link between TZP IFS rules and the spherical-Bessel modal content of the initial field. - Bifurcation at TZP corresponds to passing a threshold in modal energy for a set of \((\ell,k)\) such that the tidal eigenvalues cross the collapse thresholds in (3). The mathematical condition is precisely when mode-weighted sums of second derivatives (tidal tensor entries) exceed \(1/D(t)\).
theory.technical_interpretation: How This Ties to TZP & Your 32/27 Scale Intuition: A(k)∝ r^α(k) is a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation, written A(k)∝ r^α(k). It expresses a scaling or proportionality, capturing how the quantity grows with its parameters. It is built from α, A, k, r. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where that ratio can be introduced as a scale-selection operator on mode amplitudes: e.g., apply amplitude weighting
